I've been very happy with my Samsung 65" Crystal UHD U7900FD 4K Smart TV. The picture quality is sharp and vibrant, with excellent color and clarity whether I'm watching movies, sports, or streaming my favorite shows. The smart TV interface is easy to navigate, and apps load quickly.

The reason I'm giving it 4 stars instead of 5 is that the built-in speakers are decent but could provide a little more depth and bass. Also, some settings took a bit of time to customize to my preferences.

Overall, this TV offers great value for the price, delivers an impressive viewing experience, and has been a fantastic addition to my home entertainment setup. I would definitely recommend it to others looking for a quality 4K smart TV.

title: Bolt holder no good on the back of the TVs
text: So I had a technician come out us do a service call at my house. He called his company and he took the back of the TV off and one of the bolts that came with the wall mount. There’s a third place out of the four bolts that attach it to the wall that runs right into a power strip he said he’s never seen anything like that before. It’s a really bad model that he can’t believe that the TV is being sold still he called Samsung. They said they’d replace the back of the TV where the bolt holes are three of them are only threaded for a bolt. The other one is just a plastic hole that you can’t put the boat all the way through into or it will hit a power strip and cause the screen not to work or other things function. Still haven’t received my back of my TV that needs replaced. His name was Joe was the tech that came out. I’ve been waiting for several days with no answer, Samsung needs to do better. I heard him on the phone and they said that they would replace the back of the TV and now when I called them, they’re telling me that I have to call Samsung directly instead of them, which is it’s already been okayed so I was just left. Wondering until I reached out to the service tech and would’ve never received my back and I still probably won’t.

title: Sound is AWFUL
text: Initially I was happy with my purchase, the colors are bold,  bright, with resolution is sharp.  TV functionality can be slow at times. I like that I can watch local news stations, that was a cool discovery after I purchased my TV. But approximately 3 months after my purchase, the speakers in the TV sound busted! The sound is awful at times. Voices vibrate and sound robotic at times. Background noise and music is even worse. It’s odd because it’s not constant and the volume of my TV never changes, so I’m not sure what caused the issue. While the speakers don’t sound distorted, they do majority of the time, it’s so distracting and hard to watch at times. I’m so disappointed, I’ll never buy another Samsung TV. Don’t waste your money on something you’ll likely have to replace sooner than later. I gave this TV a 2 because the speakers should have lasted longer than 3 months. Had my speakers not broken I probably would have rated this TV a 4.

title: All around great tv
text: I am very happy with our decision to purchase our Samsung TV. While there are lower cost options available for the same size television, Samsung is still competitively priced, and the quality is much better. The picture is very crisp and clear. Also, we’ve noticed that the sound quality on our other tv (not the Samsung) is very low and requires the use of a soundbar. The sound quality on our Samsung is excellent, and doesn’t need any enhancement. The remote control is very user friendly and the responsiveness to the controls is quick. Once our other tv needs to be replaced, we will be purchasing another Samsung.

title: Great picture and price. Bad AI integration.
text:
Everything about the picture is top notch. The clarity, brightness, resolution. The vibrancy makes me feel like I'm in the movie or show.  The negatives are the home screen setup and the overall settings. 
The home screen has too much mindless filler and the apps I've downloaded aren't front and center. The search (for shows/movies is too convoluted with YouTube and ad options before you get to the show/movie.
The settings of the TV (picture, sound, captions, etc.) are hard to find once in the settings app. Things aren't where my common sense would tell me they would be. The remote control has too few functioning buttons and too many that do nothing. 
Overall a great TV but Samsung has put too much AI suggestions into everything.

title: Network tends to drop while streaming in apps
text: This TV is aesthetically quite beautiful and has great resolution. I love the image quality and how quick and easy it was to set up and link to the Samsung phones in my house with the SmartThings app. The brightness gets quite bright which is great because my living room it is in is very dark later in the day but I will say that if I have the recessed light turned on just above the mounted TV, it tends to wash out the image and create a glare (as lights tend to do). The colors are bright and vivid on the picture and I feel as though my Samsung Account is secure being linked to the TV. My one main complaint is that every so often (about once every 2 hours) I lose my network connection. Sometimes it is only for a second or 2 before it reconnect and other times, I have to either change the channel on whatever app Im using or I will have to back completely out and restart the app I'm using. I have sat and waited for it to reconnect and if I'm in Samsung TV Plus, it will at times not go back to the programming unless I physically click a button... which isnt too big of a hassle, it just feels unnecessary as an older Samsung TV I used to have was capable of never losing my network connection. (And I want to mention that I have made sure it was not because of my home internet provider and have the router directly underneath of the TV and have used all different Ghz wifis to try and make it stop with no change.) Overall, it is a solid TV that I enjoy. The sound could be a bit louder for such a large TV but I still am very happy with this TV and would recommend it to friends and family.

title: Won't link to any wifi

I recently purchased this new TV replacement that had broken on my outside patio I was a little taken back by the new style remote but being I have a roku TV I was able to figure it out quite easily.  The problem I have is my tv will not pick up any Wi-Fi anywhere so as it stands, the only thing my TV picks up is digital TV from the antenna will not pick up any internet so I can't get any of my Samsung channels, my Netflix or any other apps that I have set up for my other televisions and it's very discouraging, and I can't figure out why I've done every steps.  That I've seen to get remedy of this situation, but nothing seems to happen.I just wish someone could give me a hand with this.So I could get this t v working like I wanted to.

title: Stunning 75" Samsung 4K TV Picture Quality
text:
5 out of 5 stars
Excellent picture quality that really impressed me. I picked up this 75" Samsung Crvsta UHD U7900F 4K TV a few weeks ago and it's been a huge upgrade for my living room. The size is perfect for movie nights and sport. It feels immersive without being overwhelming.The aesthetics are really clean and modern. The slim bezels and sleek black finish look premium on my media console, and it blends in nicely with the room instead of standing out like an eyesore.Brightness is outstanding. Even with lights on in the room during the day, everything stays clear and punchy. I don't have to close the curtains anymore to enjoy a good show.Contrast is also impressive for a UHD TV in this range. Blacks look deep and inky, especially in darker scenes from movies like Dune or The Batman. Details in shadows don't get lost. Resolution is sharp as expected from 4K. Text, fine details, and textures all pop. You can really see the difference when watching 4K content on Netflix or YouTube.What surprised me the most is the vibrancy. Colors are rich and lively without looking over-saturated or fake. Skin tones look natural, greens in nature documentaries are vivid, and reds really stand out. Everything just looks more alive.Overall, this TV delivers fantastic picture performance for the price. Highly recommend if you're looking for a big-screen 4K that makes your favorite content look great.

title: Excellent Picture Quality and Outstanding Value
text:
I recently purchased the Samsung 55" Class Crystal UHD U7900F 4K TV, and I could not be happier with my decision. From the moment I turned it on, the picture quality was impressive. The colors are vibrant, the image is crisp and clear, and the 4K resolution brings movies, sports, and streaming content to life.

The setup process was straightforward and user-friendly. Within minutes, I was connected to my Wi-Fi and accessing all of my favorite streaming apps. The smart TV interface is intuitive and responsive, making it easy to navigate between apps and content.

I am especially impressed with how well this TV performs in both bright and dark rooms. The Crystal UHD technology delivers excellent contrast and detail, creating an immersive viewing experience. Whether I am watching a blockbuster movie, live sports, or the evening news, the picture remains consistently sharp and vibrant.

The design is sleek and modern, fitting perfectly into my living room without taking up unnecessary space. The sound quality is also surprisingly good for a television in this price range.

Samsung continues to deliver exceptional quality, and this TV offers tremendous value for the price. If you are looking for a reliable, beautiful, and feature-rich 4K television, I highly recommend the Samsung 55" Class Crystal UHD U7900F.This version reads like a genuine consumer review while highlighting the features most shoppers look for when making a purchase decision.

title: Great features
text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Love This TV – Highly Recommend!
"I’ve been using my Samsung Smart TV for a month and I am absolutely thrilled with it.
What I love most:
 Amazing Picture Quality: The colors are incredibly vibrant, and the picture is crystal clear whether I'm watching movies, sports, or regular shows.
 Super Smart & Easy to Use: Setting it up was a breeze. I love the built-in interface because all of my favorite apps (like Netflix, YouTube, and Disney+) are right there and load instantly.
 Sleek Design: It looks beautiful and modern in my living room, with a very slim border around the screen.
Any minor downsides?
The sound from the TV speakers is decent, but I ended up pairing it with a soundbar to get that true movie-theater feel.
Overall, Samsung made a fantastic TV. If you are looking for great quality and a smooth smart TV experience, this is definitely worth every penny!"

title: Stunning Picture Quality  	•	4K Resolution: Offers
text:
The new Samsung 58” TV has truly transformed our short-term vacation rental! Its sleek, modern design adds a touch of elegance to the space, seamlessly blending with the decor and enhancing the overall aesthetic. Guests have commented on how visually appealing it is, making it a standout feature of the living area.

In terms of performance, this TV excels. The brightness and color vibrancy are remarkable, bringing movies and shows to life with stunning clarity. Whether it’s a sunny day or a cozy evening, the picture remains sharp and immersive, ensuring that guests enjoy an exceptional viewing experience. The resolution is fantastic, making even the smallest details pop, which has been a huge hit with visitors.

Security is also a priority for us as hosts, and this Samsung model offers peace of mind. With features like guest mode, we can ensure that personal settings are protected, allowing guests to enjoy their stay without compromising our privacy. This thoughtful design element adds to the overall appeal, making it a worry-free addition to our rental.

In summary, the Samsung 58” TV not only elevates the aesthetics of our space but also delivers outstanding picture quality while providing security for our guests. It’s a fantastic investment that has enhanced our rental experience!

Bill Roberson

title: The Best TVs!
text:
The 65" Crystal UHD U7900FD 4K Smart TV has exceeded my expectations in every way. From the moment it was installed, its sleek, modern design added a premium look to my living room. The slim profile and minimalist bezel create a clean, elegant aesthetic that complements any space.
The picture quality is outstanding. The 4K resolution delivers exceptional sharpness and detail, while the brightness levels ensure a clear, vivid image even in well-lit rooms. Colors are incredibly vibrant and lifelike, making movies, sports, and streaming content more immersive and enjoyable. The contrast and clarity bring out details that are often missed on lower-quality displays.
I also appreciate the smart features and built-in security protections, which provide peace of mind when connecting apps and streaming services. Navigation is intuitive, responsive, and easy to use.
Overall, this TV offers an excellent combination of style, performance, security, and value. If you're looking for a television with stunning visuals, vibrant color reproduction, impressive brightness, and a sophisticated design, the U7900FD is an outstanding choice.

I recently purchased the Samsung 75" Class Crystal UHD U7900F 4K Smart TV (2025), and I couldn’t be happier with it. The Crystal UHD processor does a great job making everything look crisp, and the smart TV features are easy to use and navigate. Streaming apps load quickly, and the interface is smooth and responsive. The design is also sleek and modern, which looks great in my living room.

For the price, this TV delivers outstanding value. If you're looking for a large-screen TV with beautiful colors, impressive picture quality, and a great smart TV experience, I highly recommend the Samsung U7900F. It's been a fantastic addition to my home entertainment setup.

title: Great picture
text:
“After using the Samsung 55" Crystal UHD 4K Smart TV for a while now, I can honestly say I’m really impressed with everything this TV offers for the price. The picture quality is beautiful — the colors are vibrant, the blacks look deep, and everything looks incredibly sharp in 4K. Watching movies and shows feels so much more immersive, especially with the Crystal UHD display and HDR. Even older content looks better because the upscaling feature really helps improve the picture quality.
One of my favorite things is how easy the smart features are to use. The Tizen system runs smoothly, apps load quickly, and streaming services like Netflix, Hulu, Disney+, and YouTube are all super convenient to access. The menu is clean and simple, which made setup quick and stress-free.
The sound quality also surprised me. The audio is clear, loud, and balanced without needing a soundbar right away. I also really like how sleek and modern the TV looks — the thin design and minimal bezels make it look much more expensive than it actually is.
For gaming, movies, sports, or everyday streaming, this TV does an amazing job. The motion handling is smooth, and everything looks crisp without much blur. Overall, I feel like Samsung did a great job creating a budget-friendly 4K TV that still feels premium. Definitely one of the best purchases I’ve made for my home entertainment setup.”
The TV has strong customer ratings and features like 4K upscaling, Motion Xcelerator, HDR, and Samsung’s Tizen smart platform.

title: Buyer **BEWARE**
text:
Really was excited for this tv seeing how it was a great price. My old Vizio tv just so happened to have dead led bars at the top after a good solid 5/6 years. This tv went on sale at Walmart and I was genuinely happy to go with a Samsung because of the reputation they have and whatnot. 

First impression out the box has great resolution, tv is a great size, and for the most part the interface is semi-fast. It does show lag compared to my old Vizio and things tend to take a glitchy sec to load. No biggie, still fast enough to not bother. 

After setting up my tv I did notice one stuck pixel. Just one single stuck greenish/white pixel that you can only see in dark movies or during screen transitions. Definitely noticed behind any dark background. I attempted to run the pixel stuck video on YouTube, but that was not successful. After that I went on to let Walmart know, all they can do is refund it since the deal is off. Reach out to Samsung customer support, provide many pictures of this stuck pixel and have about a 45 min conversation with a rep to come to the conclusion that he will send a repair man to my house to fix it. However, since I was not at home to connect my camera through my phone so he can get a visual on my tv it had to wait. 30 min later I come back to Samsung support and I now have a new rep who does not pick up where the conversation left off, instead has me do troubleshooting. 

Unplug all HDMI unplug tv 3 min and plug tv back in. Still stuck pixel. Next we do a picture diagnostic that comes with the tv found in the settings. It shows a picture of a cheeseburger and since he cannot see the pixel in that picture he decided that a repair man is not needed. “If more pixels become stuck or dead then we can continue with a repair” is what is told to me. A literally 2 week out the box TV and no resolution. 

I am highly disappointed with how this was handled, no resolution to fix this issue and just kind of blown off. My advice- if you see anything wrong with your Samsung TV return it that day. This TV cost way too much money for this to happen and more than likely I will haul this 75” TV back to Walmart. 

Super disappointed in the customer supports resolution and has turned into such a hassle.

title: REQUEST FOR ASSISTANCE.
text: Following my purchase of my Samsung TV, I purchased a Polk Surround System from a Dish  technician that assisted with the activation of my new Television. Shortly after that date, one of the small sub-woofers would not connect to the soundbar when the TV was turned on. The only way to get it to come om was to unplug it and then reinstall the plug. Whenever the Samsung was turned off, it would again fail to connect. Despite two return trips by the Dish representative, the problem continues. The circuits have been tested, speaker moved to different locations and the problem continues. Is there any setting within the Samsung that could require adjustment to allow both small sub-woofers to connect at the same time. Probably reaching for straws, but, thought I would at least ask. Thanks for hopefully a response.

title: Great Picture
text:
The Samsung 55" Crystal UHD TV delivers an impressive viewing experience with stunning picture quality and vibrant colors that truly bring movies, shows, and games to life. The display is bright and clear, making it easy to watch during the day, while still providing excellent visibility and contrast in darker rooms.
One of the standout features of this TV is how balanced the picture looks in any lighting condition. Whether you’re watching a late-night movie or streaming your favorite show in a bright living room, the screen remains sharp, colorful, and easy on the eyes. The Crystal UHD technology enhances detail and color accuracy, creating a more immersive experience overall.
In addition to the great visuals, the TV is user-friendly and performs smoothly. The setup is simple, and navigating apps and menus is quick and easy. For anyone looking for a reliable 55" TV with beautiful picture quality at a great value, the Samsung Crystal UHD is an excellent choice.

title: Great tv
text:
This is our first smart tv other than using at fire stick. The picture and resolution on this tv are beautiful. The picture is bright and very clear and clean. The install and setup was fairly simple.  It is excellent for our Xbox and switch. The actual tv is very simple and matches any decor. 

If I had any minor complaints, it would be that the os is very restrictive as far as what we can install and use. I understand the security that is involved, but it would be nice if more apps were available. Even if it was “use at your own risk.”

Overall we are very pleased with our choice of a Samsung smart tv and would definitely purchase another one the next time we are in the market.

title: Stunning picture quality
text:
An Absolute Game-Changer Home Entertainment!
I couldn't be happier with my **Samsung 75-inch TV**. 
If you are looking to bring the true theater experience right into your living room, this is exactly how you do it.
### What Makes It Stand Out:
 * **Stunning Picture Quality:** The clarity, vibrant colors, and deep contrasts make everything look spectacular. Whether it’s an action-packed movie, a live sporting event, or a sweeping nature documentary, the visual detail is sharp and incredibly lifelike.
 * **Massive, Immersive Screen:** Upgrading to the 75-inch size completely changes how you watch TV. It fills your field of view perfectly, making you feel like you're part of the action without overwhelming the room.
 * **User-Friendly Smart Features:** Samsung’s smart platform is fast, smooth, and incredibly intuitive. Navigating between streaming apps like Netflix, YouTube, and live TV is seamless, and the setup was an absolute breeze.
 * **Sleek Design:** Despite its massive size, the TV has a beautiful, slim profile and thin bezels that look great on the wall or on a media console.
### The Verdict
Samsung has delivered a premium viewing experience with excellent reliability. It has turned our living room into the ultimate gathering spot for movie nights and the big game. If you’re on the fence about going big—**do it**. It is worth every single penny!

title: A great TV!
text:
I recently picked up the Samsung 65" U7900FD Crystal UHD TV, and I am seriously impressed. The 4K picture quality is absolutely stunning—everything is incredibly sharp, and the Crystal Processor does a fantastic job upscaling older content so it looks crisp. The colors are super vibrant and lifelike, and when you watch anything in HDR10+, the contrast and depth really make the scenes pop.
​The design is another huge plus. It has this sleek, premium "MetalStream" frame with practically no bezels, so it looks amazing on the wall and just blends right into the room without being distracting. Plus, the Tizen smart platform is incredibly smooth and easy to navigate. Having quick access to all my favorite streaming apps, the Gaming Hub for cloud gaming, and a ton of free channels on Samsung TV Plus right out of the box is awesome.
​Even the little details are great, like the built-in SmartThings hub for managing smart home devices right from the screen and the Knox security keeping everything protected. If you want a massive, gorgeous 4K display with high-end features and a great interface without dropping thousands on a flagship, this TV is an absolute home run.

title: Exceeds Modern Expectations in Features
text:
The Samsung Crystal UHD 50-inch 4K Smart TV delivered a very good viewing experience overall. 
The design was sleek and modern with thin bezels that looked clean in almost any room setup, and the 4K picture quality was sharp with vibrant colors that made movies like Hobbit look impressive. Brightness levels were strong enough for daytime viewing, while darker scenes still maintained decent detail and contrast. The smart features were also convenient and easy to use. I especially liked the ability to use Bluetooth connectivity, smartphone remote functions, and private audio through headphones connected to my phone. Replacement remotes were inexpensive and easy to find, which made having multiple remotes around the house convenient.
From a Security and software standpoint, the interface felt stable and straightforward, and I never had major issues with apps, connectivity, or navigation while the TV was functioning properly. For several years it performed exactly as expected and was genuinely enjoyable to use daily. The only thing worth mentioning is long-term durability. My particular unit eventually developed issues over time before finally failing completely, so while the features and picture quality were excellent throughout ownership, it may not be the kind of television built for multi-generation longevity, although that seems fairly common with most modern electronics today. Still, during the years it worked properly, it delivered a premium-looking image, useful smart features, and an overall user-friendly experience that made it easy to enjoy every day. This TV actually replaced another Samsung that eventually failed as well, and despite that, I would still gladly purchase another Samsung in the future when my current replacement eventually reaches the end of its lifespan in a few years.

title: The Ultimate Viewing Experience Every Time
text:
I’ve owned Samsung TVs for years, and once again they exceeded my expectations. The picture quality is absolutely stunning — the brightness, color accuracy, and resolution make every movie, sports event, and video look incredibly realistic and immersive. The colors are vibrant without looking artificial, blacks are deep, and the overall image clarity is simply outstanding.
The design is also very elegant and modern, adding a premium look to my living room. Everything feels high-end and well built, exactly what I expect from Samsung products. Navigation is smooth, the smart features work flawlessly, and the overall performance is fast and reliable.
Another thing I really appreciate is the security and stability of the system. Samsung continues to provide a trustworthy and polished experience that feels premium in every detail.
After trying many TVs over the years, Samsung remains my favorite brand by far. Amazing technology, excellent build quality, and a viewing experience that truly stands out. Highly recommended!

title: My Samsung TV
text:
I recently purchased the Samsung 50" Crystal UHD U7900F, and I have to say—this TV exceeded my expectations in just about every way.
First, the picture quality is outstanding. The 4K resolution is crisp and vibrant, with colors that really pop without looking over-processed. Whether I’m watching movies, sports, or streaming shows, everything looks clean, sharp, and immersive. Samsung’s Crystal Processor does a great job upscaling lower-resolution content, making even older shows look noticeably better. �
Best Buy
The design is sleek and modern, with a slim profile that fits perfectly in my space. It doesn’t feel bulky at all, and it adds a nice, clean aesthetic to the room.
I also really appreciate the smart features. The built-in Tizen OS makes it easy to access all my favorite apps—Netflix, YouTube, Prime Video, and more—without needing extra devices. Navigation is smooth, and everything loads quickly. �
Samsung
Another big plus is the value for the price. This TV delivers a premium viewing experience without the premium price tag. It’s perfect for anyone who wants excellent performance without stepping into OLED or QLED price ranges.
If I had to mention one minor drawback, it would be that the refresh rate is standard (60Hz), so it’s not ideal for hardcore gamers—but for everyday viewing, it’s more than enough.

title: might be better off saving your money
text:
I'm not sure if it's a software problem but whatever operating system is on this TV is very slow and laggy even with the best of the best internet I've tried it on fiber and I've also tried it with the most expensive star link plan there's no difference there that's how I know it's the TV and not a internet connection.

The interface itself also is very disappointing there's all these recommended garbage things that I don't want and I actually have to scroll down through the trash to find my actual apps or the actual features that I'm looking for which is very inconvenient for me I like to be able to turn on a TV and go instantly to where I want kind of like the Roku settings are. So keep that in mind when you're designing other things.

There's also an issue with a lot of the pre-downloaded programs. They will not let you delete them you can go through the whole deleting process and you can click okay to delete and it still stays there as well as new things you download you have to delete them three maybe five times before they'll actually be removed from your TV.

The lack of an input section is fairly confusing I'm young so I was able to figure it out pretty quickly but for an elderly person I do not think this thing would be a good choice.

I also don't like the fact that Samsung monitors everything I play on the TV for advertisement purposes and sells that data that's very bothersome and disturbing to me and if I would have known that before I spent the money I would not have purchased this TV. I am sick of being tracked with every little thing I do!

Honestly if this TV wasn't so difficult to hang I would have returned it because of what I have described above.

title: Perfect TV for that home theatre
text:
The Samsung U7900FD Crystal UHD TV (2025) is a great option if you want a solid 4K TV without spending a lot. It’s easy to use, looks good, and delivers a really enjoyable everyday viewing experience.

The picture quality is clear and sharp, with nice, natural colors. Whether you’re watching movies, sports, or streaming shows, everything looks crisp and smooth. It also does a good job of improving lower-quality content so it still looks great on a 4K screen.

The smart features are simple and fast. Apps load quickly, and it’s easy to switch between them. You also get access to plenty of free content, which is a nice bonus.

Design-wise, the TV is slim with thin bezels, so it looks modern and fits well in almost any room.

Overall, this TV does exactly what most people need—it’s reliable, easy to use, and delivers great picture quality for the price.

title: Perfect Family TV with Stunning Picture
text:
I have been extremely impressed with this 75 inch Crystal UHD TV. The picture quality is outstanding right out of the box. Colors are vibrant, blacks are deep, and everything looks incredibly sharp whether I am watching movies, sports, or streaming shows. The brightness is strong enough for daytime viewing and still looks great at night.
The size makes a huge difference in the overall experience. It really feels like a home theater in my living room. Fast moving scenes stay smooth and clear, which is perfect for sports and action movies. The sound is also better than expected for a flat screen, but pairing it with a sound system takes it to another level.
One feature that really stands out for me as a parent is the parental controls. With young kids in the house, it is important to manage what they can access, and this TV makes that simple. I can easily restrict content, control app access, and have peace of mind knowing they are only watching age appropriate material.
The smart features are simple to use and responsive. Apps load quickly and navigating through menus is easy even for someone who is not very tech savvy. Setup was straightforward and took very little time.
Overall, this TV delivers excellent value for the price. Between the picture quality, size, ease of use, and family friendly controls, it exceeded my expectations. I would absolutely recommend it to anyone looking to upgrade their home entertainment setup.

title: More Than a Display—It’s an Aesthetic Experience
text:
​When I decided to upgrade my home entertainment setup, I wasn't just looking for a screen; I was looking for a centerpiece. The Samsung 65" Crystal UHD 4K Smart TV hasn’t just filled the space on my wall—it has completely redefined how I engage with visual storytelling.
​Visual Brilliance meets Tailored Design
The first thing that strikes you is the MetalStream design. In a world of bulky plastic, this TV feels like an intentional piece of architecture. It’s sleek, minimal, and blends seamlessly with my home decor, proving that technology can be as beautiful as the art it displays. The Crystal Processor 4K is the true workhorse here; the 4K upscaling is genuinely transformative, pulling detail out of older classics that I’ve seen a dozen times but never truly seen until now.
​Intuitive Intelligence
What truly elevates this experience is the Tizen-powered interface. It’s snappy, intuitive, and doesn't get in the way of what matters. Whether I am jumping from a high-stakes gaming session using the Auto Game Mode or settling in for a cinematic night with HDR-enhanced content, the transition is effortless. The adaptive brightness is a standout feature—it respects the lighting in my room, ensuring that whether it’s high noon or late evening, the picture remains perfectly balanced without me ever having to touch a setting.
​The Bottom Line
This TV is the rare intersection of luxury aesthetics and high-performance engineering. It provides an immersive, vivid experience that makes every frame feel deliberate. For anyone looking to invest in a television that treats their content—and their living space—with the respect it deserves, the 65" Crystal UHD is the gold standard.

It’s fine for a bedroom TV, but nothing special. The picture quality is decent for the price 4K content looks sharp enough, and colors are okay, though not particularly vibrant. Sound is pretty average, so you might want a soundbar if you care about audio.

The biggest downside is the interface. It feels cluttered and a bit slow at times, with too many menus and apps pushed in your face. It does what it’s supposed to do, but navigating it can be a bit annoying.

Overall, it serves its purpose as a secondary TV. Not amazing, not terrible..just kind of there.

title: Great features but inconsistent picture quality
text: I’m giving this 4 out of 5 stars. The aesthetics of the unit are sleek, but the color and resolution aren't always consistent. It’s frustrating when the picture quality shifts on certain streaming apps or movies, and after trying to troubleshoot it online, we still haven't found a permanent fix. On a positive note, the security features and privacy settings are easy to navigate, which gives us peace of mind. It’s a good product, but I wish the display settings were more reliable across different types of content.

title: Decent Product for the Price
text: We were in the market for a nice TV for our bedroom. Something not too big but big enough. This TV fit that bill. My husband wanted something really vivid. Like the way you see the display in the store. After tweaking some of the settings we were able to get the color and quality we wanted. My husband does have some concern about the side view when watching. He feels that yiu don't get that same vividness unless watching it straight on. But the cost was worth it for me. We have been satisfied with the way it fits into our room and in general the color, picture and resolution. I like but don't like how it connects directly to my Samsung account.  I'm not sure what the real advantage is for that, besides allowing for easy registration.

title: What a Find!
text: Great Product - Great Value. I had two "older" Android TVs literally "die" within hours of each other and found myself in need of a new television. My main TV was a 50 inch, and I knew I needed a larger screen. While looking at the options at Costco, I came across the 65 Inch Samsung Class Crystal UHD U7900FD 4K Smart TV. Under $350. Perfect size (NOT AN ANDROID) and loved all the features. The set-up and unpacking were a Breeze - (minutes). It was extremely light weight compared to the Sony I replaced. Literally still learning all of the features, but you won't be disappointed.

title: Good TV, But May Not Work With Your Cable.
text: The TV has a great picture and the sound is good, too.  However, we have found it more difficult to use compared to our 2017 similar Samsung model.  We have to use the "antenna" setting to get our cable channels via coax at our complex.  We ended up getting a HDMI connected cable box to see these channels as the TV's tuner would not let us change channels. When we tried to change a manual entered channel, we were put in the plus channels.  It was a slow process to just see the local channels.  Also, while there was a channel guide, but the TV was unable to display any of the information.  At least the channel numbers came up.  Our older TV did not have any problems like these.  I know these new sets have many, many advanced features, but for someone who needs the basic, it did not meet our needs and is frustrating to use.  Samsung's support is excellent and helpful, but they did not have a clue how to help.  Maybe our situation is unique, but we have had our enjoyment diminished because of having to go to an external streaming boxes to get the channels we want to watch.

title: Great Tv
text:
The Samsung U75 TV is a breathtaking centerpiece for any living room! From the moment you turn it on, the picture quality absolutely blows you away. The colors are incredibly vibrant, the contrast is deep, and the ultra-high definition resolution delivers crystal-clear details that make movies, sports, and gaming truly immersive.
​The sleek, practically bezel-less design looks fantastic whether it's on a stand or mounted on the wall, giving the room a premium, modern feel. On the software side, Samsung’s smart TV interface is snappy, intuitive, and packed with all the streaming apps you could ever need right out of the box.

title: Great price, disappointing viewing
text: It’s a large screen TV at a great price. My primary quarrel is with watching dark shows (so many are these days) during the daytime in a well lit room. It proves nearly impossible to see any detail. It’s unfortunate and what that means is I can only watch my primary shows during the evening. TV is a serious hobby and I go to sleep fairly early so it’s upsetting to be confined to evenings only which provides only 1-2 of quality watch time. Unfortunately there is not enough adjustment within the TV’s picture capabilities to combat this though I’ve optimized settings as much as possible. I also had an issue with weird red lines appearing onscreen occasionally. I had to unplug the TV to make them go away. This issue seems to have resolved after 3-4 months.

title: Best quality
text:
I recently purchased this 85-inch TV, and it has completely transformed my viewing experience. The picture quality is absolutely stunning—crisp, vibrant colors with deep blacks and impressive brightness that make everything from movies to sports look incredibly lifelike. The size alone gives it a true theater feel right in my living room.

The sound quality is surprisingly good for a TV this size, but pairing it with a soundbar takes it to another level. Setup was quick and straightforward, and the smart features are smooth and easy to navigate. Streaming apps load fast, and I haven’t experienced any lag.

What really stands out is how immersive everything feels—whether I’m watching movies, gaming, or just streaming shows, it pulls you right in. Definitely a great value for the size and performance.

Overall, I highly recommend this TV to anyone looking to upgrade their home entertainment setup. It’s a big investment, but absolutely worth it.

title: Horrible Product and Horrible Service.
text: Sound gets extremely distorted. TV is six months old. The more background noise in a show, game or movie the worse it gets. And it gets worse the longer the TV is on. It is at every volume level. We have over 40 videos of the problem showing volume levels and date stamps. Samsung service is ridiculous. It took days to get a real person. Even if they send a technician from a service company to look at the TV, the technician can only call into another service desk. The tech does not interact with you. He just listens to the person on the phone tell him to check the setting. Samsung decided we must be turning the volume up too loud. Never asked us that, but ignored the videos we took showing the volume level at 15 to 20. They refuse service on purpose. and run you through hours and hours to ask the same questions. Have you take off of work for almost complete days at a time, making it so inconvenient for you that you cannot help but to give up. We will never purchase another Samsung product again. Conveniently, I cannot upload one of my videos for proof since it would violate the copyrighted media policy and my review would be deleted, per the Samsung policy when you try to attach a video.

title: Great Smart Tv
text:
bought the 75-inch Samsung Crystal UHD U7900F 4K Smart TV in 2025, and I’m extremely happy with it. Everything about this TV has been great so far. The screen size is perfect and really makes the viewing experience feel more immersive.
The smart features are easy to use, the apps run smoothly, and the menu is simple to navigate. I also like how sleek and modern the TV looks in my living room. It has a very clean design and feels like a premium product for the price.
Sound quality is good, setup was easy, and overall performance has been excellent. For the money, this TV is a fantastic value. I would definitely recommend it to anyone looking for a big, beautiful, and reliable 4K smart TV.

title: I love my new tv
text:
​"I recently upgraded to the 75-inch U7900FD and honestly, the size alone is a game-changer. For a 'Crystal UHD' (not the super expensive OLEDs), the picture is surprisingly sharp and the colors really pop, especially when watching 4K content. The setup was quick, and the Tizen smart interface is easy to navigate.
​The Pros: Huge 75" display for a great price, sleek 'MetalStream' design, and decent upscaling for older shows.
The Cons: It only has 2 HDMI ports, so if you have a soundbar and multiple consoles, you'll need a hub. Also, the remote is a bit basic (no voice control).
Overall: 4.5/5. If you want a theater feel without spending thousands, this is the one."

title: Spend more, buy better
text: One of the first things you notice once you get the TV setup is the remote.  The lack of number buttons and no microphone. This makes it a hassle to change channels.  When you use the channel up/down button it takes you to the Samsung plus app even when you're watching antenna TV.  Syncing the audio to the video is impossible for any length of time.  When you get it synced for one channel, it's off on other channels.  The two ten watt speakers are extremely under powered. You have to have the TV volume at half or better to hear most channels, and it won't go high enough to hear others. Samsung plus has a nasty habit of locking up, freezing,  for several seconds at a time and usually in the most inopertune moments.  This means you miss important scenes or happenings in the program you're watching. No microphone! The remote would be easier to tolerate if you could at least command the TV by voice. The menu seems clunky and not very well laid out or labeled. Pluses include that the TV starts quickly and has vibrant color.

title: Need to improve user experience features.
text: I am very disappointed with the remote. It is severely limited in terms of controlling a number of aspects. I just don't understand the consistent move to try to make the remotes so small and in the process eliminate a number of great features. It is nearly impossible to control recordings with the standard Samsung remove for example. It also seems fairly difficult to add Directv streaming to the regular streaming services. The UI for regular people is very important!!

title: Beware of this TV
text: I have encountered several issues with this television. Firstly, when attempting to transmit audio from the television to a connected Bluetooth speaker, I experienced static interference, necessitating a power cycle of the television to resolve the issue. This problem was not isolated to a single Samsung speaker but occurred with other speakers as well. Secondly, approximately 30 minutes into viewing movies, persistent glitches appeared. Thirdly, the television's remote control exhibits a noticeable delay in responsiveness when navigating through various applications and games. I am dissatisfied with this purchase and would not recommend this product, despite owning several other Samsung products.

title: Horrible television and Horrible Samsung Support.
text: I purchased this television late January. It lasted exactly three weeks before it just completely stopped working. This was only the beginning of what I’m still dealing with. Samsung told me a third party company would pick up my three week old tv and fix it. Someone from that company did show up, took some pictures of it and that was it. I called them for an update and was informed Samsung would not be fixing it because the parts were too expensive. Did Samsung bother to call me?  No. I only found out that my ticket was closed because I called Samsung for an update. This was almost three weeks ago. Today, a third party company was supposed to pick up the tv that is not working anymore. They didn’t show up or even bother to call me. It’s almost going to be one month that I’ve had this huge tv just sitting on my living room floor. Samsung is going to send me a new tv (I’m regretting that decision) but won’t do it until this one is picked up. If you’re deciding on purchasing anything from Samsung, I highly recommend you do not do it. This is my first and only time ever purchasing Samsung items. This has been an absolute nightmare and I can’t wait for it to be over. Please do not purchase anything from them. If things don’t work out, it’s a nightmare to get it fixed.

I received this as an upgrade from the 43inch TV offered by Verizon.  I chose to pony up the $100 for the upgrade and it was well worth it.  This TV is very clear and I use it for a computer monitor and watching my YouTube videos as well as a few other minor things.

Zero issues with anything.  Worked right out of the box flawlessly.   Sound is not too bad at all considering it's a TV but is much better than most others I have come across.   Attaching my sound bar makes the experience even better.  4K resolution is great and exactly what is to be exected from a modern unit.   I'm not good with colors but the wife says the colors and brightness are excellent.

title: Love at First Screen...Delivers Epic Everything!
text: I absolutely love my 75-inch Samsung TV. It's vibrant and ultra-sleek in size for a TV of its kind. The resolution brings every scene to life with stunning clarity and color depth. Connectivity is fast and user friendly, especially with other Samsung devices, making setup effortless. The sound quality and color depth elevate the entire experience to premium levels. I use the SmartThings app for additional features such as self adjusting brightness and lighting control and mobile remote use via your mobile phone. There are lots of selections to choose from depending on preference. The entire entertainment value is packed within, including games, apps, and music channels. The TV remote has one push buttons to easily access YouTube and Prime if that's your thing. I also love the synchronicity of using Spotify from mobile to TV. It's a great product filled with many extras that I wasn't expecting.

title: The U7900FD: My Living Room’s Main Character
text:
My living room has officially peaked. I used to think a TV was just a black rectangle, but the Aesthetics of the U7900FD are so sleek that my old coffee table is actually starting to look embarrassed to be in the same room with it.
​The Brightness on this thing is a total game-changer, specifically because the adaptive brightness is so smart it practically has a PhD. It senses the light in the room and adjusts itself perfectly, meaning I no longer have to choose between "blinding glare" and "squinting in the dark." The Vibrancy is just as impressive; the colors pop so hard I’m constantly checking the floor to see if the pixels spilled out onto the carpet.
​When it comes to Resolution, the 4K detail is so crisp I can see the exact moment an actor begins to regret their career choices. I now know more about the pores on a CGI dragon's nose than I do about my own family. Plus, with the built-in Security features, I can binge-watch in total peace knowing my digital life is locked down tighter than a drum. If you want a TV that makes your actual reality look a bit dull by comparison, this is it.

title: Great for TV, gaming, and as a laptop monitor
text: My bedroom is also my office, and I use this TV for both business and pleasure. It makes a terrific monitor for my laptop, with super crisp definition, accurate color, and the brightness and contrast adjustments I need to make my work day easy on the eyes. It's big enough that I can open many windows to multitask on my screen. Then it transitions nicely to my evening TV and movie watching. The brights are nice and bright, the colors are vivid, the shadows are rich and dark, and the definition and refresh rate make video smooth, which I especially appreciate because I also use it for video gaming. I also make and sell t-shirts, and when I create the artwork the images have the color accuracy and detail level I need for my artwork. The TV is light and flat enough that it mounts easily on a standard wall mount, which I need in order to have a large TV in a small space. I took off a half star each for two minor inconveniences. The first is that there are some aspects of the "smart" navigation that I wish I could change, to adapt it more to my preferences. The second is that I hate the remote control - in particular the volume toggle, which I find difficult to work comfortably. I ended up replacing the remote with a compatible universal one that has buttons with a better feel. If not for those two small annoyances, I would have easily given this TV 5 stars.

title: My new favorite screen for binge watching
text:
I honestly love this TV. I mostly use it for streaming movies and shows on Netflix HBO Max Disney Plus and a bunch of other apps because movies are a huge part of my life. The picture quality is so clear and sharp that it actually made me excited to rewatch my favorite shows again.
I even started binge watching Game of Thrones all over and it feels like I am watching it for the first time. The details in the dark scenes look so much better and the bright scenes really stand out. It makes everything feel bigger and more cinematic right in my living room.
I am also a big anime fan and anime looks incredible on this screen. The colors are vibrant and smooth and the action scenes look so clean. Watching on this 55 inch TV is on a completely different level compared to my phone or laptop. I did not realize how much I was missing before.
The apps run smoothly and switching between them is super easy which makes binge watching even more fun. Overall I am genuinely happy with this TV and it has made my movie and anime time something I look forward to every day.

title: Awesome 50" Samsung – Crystal‑clear picture and su
text: I’m really impressed with this Samsung 50" TV. The picture quality is fantastic – colors look rich and natural, and 4K content is incredibly sharp, especially on movies and sports. The built‑in HDR makes dark scenes much easier to see without washing out the bright areas, which is a big upgrade from my older TV. Setup was fast and straightforward; I had it out of the box, connected to Wi‑Fi, and streaming in just a few minutes with no hassle. The smart TV interface is snappy and intuitive, and all my favorite apps were either preloaded or easy to add. Streaming, casting from my phone, and switching between devices (like my game console and streaming box) is smooth and lag‑free. The design is sleek with thin bezels, so it looks great on the wall and doesn’t dominate the room. For the price, the overall value is excellent – you get a modern 4K smart TV with strong picture quality, solid sound, and all the features most people need. I’d definitely buy this TV again and recommend it to anyone looking for a high‑quality 50" screen.

title: Disappointed with the functionality
text:
In terms of resolution and picture quality, this TV is an excellent buy. The image is sharp, bright, and very enjoyable for everyday viewing.

However, the functionality is limited. It only has two HDMI ports. In my case, one is used for the cable box and the other for my home theater system, which leaves no additional HDMI inputs available.

Another significant limitation is the app platform. The TV does not allow you to add apps beyond those offered in Samsung’s app store. I was unable to install some of the streaming services I use, such as Peacock, Max (HBO Max), and Paramount+. As a result, I now need to purchase an external streaming device like a Roku or Fire Stick — but with no available HDMI ports, that creates another challenge.

I want to emphasize that I am a big Samsung fan. I love the brand and own several Samsung products, including my phone, refrigerator, stove, and air conditioner. That’s actually why I chose this TV. I was aware of the HDMI limitation when I purchased it, but it was not clearly advertised that app availability would also be restricted by the platform.

Overall, the picture quality is very good, but the limited connectivity and restricted app ecosystem reduce its overall functionality.

title: A TV That Exceeded Every Expectation - Absolutely
text:
I can't believe I waited this long to upgrade my TV! I recently purchased the Samsung Crystal UHD 50" Smart TV, and I'm completely head over heels for it. This television has transformed my entire viewing experience, and I find myself looking for excuses to watch something just so I can admire the picture quality.
The Crystal UHD display is absolutely stunning. The clarity and sharpness are incredible – I'm noticing details in my favorite shows and movies that I never saw before. Colors pop with such vibrancy and richness that everything feels more immersive and lifelike. Watching nature documentaries has become a whole new experience; it's like looking through a window rather than at a screen.
The 50-inch size is perfect for my living room. It's large enough to feel cinematic without overwhelming the space. Whether I'm sitting on the couch or cooking in the adjacent kitchen, the viewing angles are excellent and the picture remains crisp from every spot in the room.
The smart TV features are incredibly intuitive and user-friendly. Setting it up was a breeze, and I had all my streaming apps running within minutes. The interface is smooth and responsive – no lag, no frustration. I love that I can seamlessly switch between Netflix, Disney+, YouTube, and my other favorite platforms without any hassle.
The sound quality genuinely surprised me too. I was expecting to need a soundbar immediately, but the built-in speakers are actually quite impressive for everyday viewing. Dialogue is clear, and there's a nice depth to the audio that makes movies feel more engaging.
For the price point, this TV is an absolute steal. The value I'm getting is exceptional – it performs like TVs that cost significantly more. I've recommended it to three friends already, and I'll continue singing its praises to anyone who will listen.
If you're on the fence about this Samsung Crystal UHD, just go for it. You won't regret it!

title: Constant Image Twitching “Critical Flaws”
text:
I love Samsung products but this tv isn't the best Constant Image Twitching Ruins the Experience
The Samsung U7900F is a disappointing TV overall. It’s best suited for watching shows in a light-controlled room, yet it still struggles there. It isn’t bright enough to handle glare, and its black levels are sub-par with no local dimming, so it also performs poorly in dark rooms.
Color performance is limited, as it can’t display a wide color gamut, though its SDR accuracy out of the box is decent. Gamers may appreciate the low input lag, which allows for responsive gameplay, but motion handling is weak, with noticeable blur behind fast-moving objects.
On the plus side, its image processing is acceptable, meaning low-resolution and low-quality content is upscaled reasonably well. Unfortunately, this isn’t enough to compensate for its major flaws, making the overall viewing experience underwhelming.

title: Gift that was no worth it  & the remote sucks
text: So my daughter purchased this TV for me as an early Christmas present when mine died. Purchased  11/23/25. The TV never seemed quite right, but I chalked it up to it being different than the one that I had for the past 15 years. The remote is absolutely crappy so I am still using the remote control from my old Samsung. But that’s not the worst of it, for the past several days my screen has been flickering. I unplugged everything plugged everything back in and now nothing comes on my screen. There’s a brief light that shows up and the screen is damaged. The TV sits 42 inches above the ground on a glass table between two bookcases and pushed back to the wall. I have no kids and my dogs certainly cannot reach this. They are trying to tell me that it’s contact damage. Well it’s funny because nobody has touched the TV since it was installed a month and a half ago. The extended warranty won’t handle it because it’s still under manufacturers warranty. And upon looking for recall information, there is no specific recall, but there are plenty of people talking about screens becoming damaged without any contact. Just by normal use because the bezel is not properly installed.  So now I have to wait two business days to get some sort of information. I have had nothing but Samsung TVs for 30 years. I have never had a problem like this and if this is not resolved, I will never purchase another Samsung TV.

title: Picture goes haywire every now and then.
text: Quite frankly I'm a bit concerned. Since I purchased this TV Occasionally I would turn it on And the color And pitcher Would be all Screwed up Looking like Smurf Ville . When I would go into settings And reboot the television this would clear up And then the picture would look normal . My problem is This has happened quite frequently Sometimes I'll go a few days With zero incident and a clear picture and then out of nowhere as I will turn it on and it's right back To a messed up picture . My concern is will this problem Get worse to the point Where Rebooting it will no longer work . And of course will this happen as soon as the manufacturer warranty Expires . I bought this open box From Best Buy And while physically everything was in great shape and it does get a great picture I have this issue . And then When I looked about getting it fixed I have to load this thing up and take it all the way to Philadelphia and drop it off For it to be covered Under Samsung Warranty. Which Moving a 65 inch television around and transporting it is no picnic . I've never had a TV in my life Have these types of issue . But I guess today with all this modern technology Crap goes wrong I paid good money for this thing and I'll be very disappointed If I turned it on one day and I am no longer able To reboot it to fix the picture issue Quite honestly I don't know what to do .

title: Poor build quality - Frame detached and ruined the
text: "I am extremely disappointed with this 75" Crystal TV. Only 19 days after purchase, the bezel (frame) started detaching from the panel due to a clear adhesive failure. This manufacturing defect caused internal pressure that eventually cracked the screen. Despite the TV being brand new and still having the original protective plastics, Samsung refused to honor the warranty, claiming 'physical damage' instead of acknowledging their structural design flaw. Do not buy if you expect durability or a fair warranty process."

title: network issues and lag on two tvs
text: Replaced two TCL smart tvs with these and I was disappointed to say the least. There is hardly and storage space on there (less than 2gb free space with just the default apps). But thats not what bothered me. The real issue is the network connectivity error I get every other time I start the tv. It's infuriating and happens all the time. It must be an issue with this model because it happens on both my tvs, not just one. It takes several restarts and/or network configurations in settings before it starts workingn again. I never had connectivity issues with my old TCLs and no other device in my home has network connection issues. To add to this, the button lag is super slow, and the home screen interface isnt very convient to get to your streaming apps easily. I was expecting more from Samung but after these go I probably wont stick with their brand.

title: Great TV
text:
I have received a free Crystal UHD U7900F 43" through Verizon. Samsung made the process quite easy and I am absolutely in love with the TV. 

It has instant startup. You can change whether it starts up on a "home" screen where you can see all the apps OR if it starts up in the app you last had it on. It can turn on your consoles by going to those channels IF you choose. If you don't want it to, you have to change the settings in your console. 

The picture is crystal clear.

The internet connection stays solid. Any connectivity issues I have were on the client side, not the TV side.

The resolution is so high that I can see every minute detail of what's being displayed, so much so that it looks like real life.

title: The brand Samsung does not care about you!
text:
This has been one of the most disappointing customer service experiences I’ve had with a major brand.

We purchased a Samsung TV for our office during the holidays. Like many businesses, the office was closed, and no one was present to receive or open the package immediately. When we finally returned and discovered an issue, we contacted Samsung—only to be told that their 5-day reporting window had passed and therefore nothing could be done.

Let me be clear: I understand policies exist. What’s unacceptable is Samsung’s complete refusal to acknowledge real-world circumstances. Holidays, office closures, and delayed access are not unusual. What is unusual is a company this large hiding behind rigid rules instead of exercising basic human judgment or customer care.

I spoke to multiple representatives, and every response was the same scripted dismissal: “There’s nothing we can do.” That’s not true. There’s plenty Samsung could do—they simply choose not to.

This experience made it painfully clear that Samsung values contracts and fine print more than customers. No empathy. No flexibility. No attempt to help. Just policy recited over and over again.

If you’re considering Samsung, know this: once your money is taken, you are on your own. If anything goes wrong outside their narrow time window—even for reasonable, understandable reasons—you will get zero support.

For a company that markets itself as premium and customer-focused, this was a complete failure.

title: I love this television for many reasons!
text:
I am so happy with our 50" Samsung Crystal television.  It was easy to set up, the picture is beautiful, and I love all the options to create the perfect settings for your environment.  We bought this for my hunny's mancave so it's nice to have the picture setting  options to adjust so we get the best picture regardless of it being bright outside or darker at night...we can always make the proper adjustments and it really helps!  I am able to watch programs without needing my glasses!  That's a first for me!  I love that I can control the settings in my Samsung Smart Things app on my phone!  Plus..it's great that I can locate my phone through my TV settings when I misplace it!  And...I seem to lose my phone more often than I'd like to admit!

The ONLY suggestion would be to make the remote easier to understand when it comes to adjusting the volume!  We both couldn't figure out that you have to push the button up and down.  It wasn't clear about how to  use it in the set-up details.  But, that's a tiny thing to manage once you figure it out.  Otherwise, we couldn't be happier!

title: Outstanding Quality
text:
I can't express how impressed I am with my new 55-inch Samsung TV. From the moment I unboxed it, the experience has been nothing short of exceptional. The setup was incredibly easy and straightforward; within minutes, we were connected to our Wi-Fi and streaming our favorite shows.
The picture quality is what truly sets this television apart. The colors are so vibrant and lifelike, and the 4K resolution is astonishingly crisp and clear. The detail is so sharp that old movies feel like new again, and streaming our favorite nature documentaries is a breathtaking experience. Even with the lights on and some glare from the window, the image remains vivid and bright.
The smart features are intuitive and seamless. Navigating between apps like Netflix, YouTube, and Amazon Prime Video is fast and responsive. The sleek, minimalist design is another huge plus; the ultra-thin bezel makes the screen feel even larger and more immersive, and when we're not watching TV, the Art Mode feature transforms it into a beautiful piece of art.
For gamers, this TV is an absolute dream. The low input lag and fast response time make for a perfectly smooth and responsive experience. Paired with its clear and crisp audio, every game feels incredibly immersive. For the price, the combination of a stunning display, excellent smart features, and easy usability makes this Samsung TV an unbeatable value. This is easily the best TV we have ever owned.

title: EXTREMELY HAPPY... HIGHLY RECOMMEND THIS TV....
text: I am extremely happy with my new Samsung TV. I replaced a 11 year old TV and could not be happier. It has so many features I love, the first of which is the picture quality and the sound quality, (they both make you feel like you are in the scene as the picture quality is so life like). Additionally, the settings for the picture and sound come in an array of options so you can adjust it to your viewing behavior or likes, and sound likes. The model I have has WiFi, and Bluetooth which are both excellent. The TV has not dropped any connections WiFi home internet connections what so ever. Additionally, the TV offers internet browsing which is also awesome. And it comes with a plethora of preinstalled apps, and you can add more apps if you have a favorite. I use the TV for online streaming of movies and occasional TV shows, as well as playing games. The TV has a library of games available to play and you can connect to online game servers. It also comes with Samsun TV which was awesome too. The quality of this TV is higher than i ever expected. Additionally, the remote is very sensitive and highly responsive. You press a button and the TV reacts without delay. I highly recommend this TV. Thanks Samsung for a high quality product.

title: Came broken and nothing done to fix it for months.
text:
Unfortunately, my TV came broken in June 2025. It is September 2025 and the broken TV is still in the box, never turned on, in my living room. It was "delivered to my home on the 13th while I was out of town, I have a covered porch so there's no reason for weather damage, not the mention the box looked undamaged. I opened the box that following weekend on the 22nd to find it has a large crack/impact on one of the corners of the screen. And began the runaround of please fix the broken TV I have been sent. From talking with customer service, I am under the impression that I was not the only person who had this problem. I was also not notified of the fifteen-day return policy until September 4th.

I was promised this tv as a promotion for joining Verizon, and though I have talked with both Samsung and Verizon, the general consensus is that Verizon says Samsung needs to send me a new one while Samsung says Verizon needs to send them a new order ticket for a replacement TV. Verizon has at least helped with my service in other ways to try to make up for the situation.

I tried to solve this before the semester started but despite having tried three times now to have the TV replaced it is still in the box waiting. As a full-time student, I cannot spend all day on the phone with not only one customer services.

I wish I had a better review of the actual TV itself.

Please note that I had until the 28th to return the TV and I contacted them on the 20th.

title: The Hands Down Best TVs!
text:
We have four Samsung TVs in our home, two 55” and two 32”. One 55” is in the Living Room for  news, sports (especially the big games), favorite shows, and Netflix, Prime etc. the other 55” is in the garage/man cave for big games, poker games and parties.  

The 32” TVs: one is in my home office/music room. I use that one for news, background music and for watching music lessons, both subscription and YouTube lessons. The other 32” is the spare bedroom for guests or when the grandkids stay over. They can watch their cartoons and play video games without disrupting the rest of the family and activities.  

We love Samsung TVs for their easy to use and manage “Smart” features. Samsung TVs are also incredibly reliable, reasonably priced, and provide great value.

title: Great experience
text:
I recently purchased the Samsung U7900F Crystal UHD TV (2025), and I am very impressed with the experience. The design is sleek and modern, with a slim frame that looks elegant in my living room.

The picture quality is outstanding – vibrant colors, deep contrast, and sharp details in both movies and sports. Even standard content looks upgraded thanks to the 4K upscaling. Motion is smooth, and fast scenes play without blurring.

The smart features are easy to use and very practical. Samsung TV Plus with free channels is a great bonus, and the TV integrates perfectly with apps like Netflix, Prime, and YouTube. It also connects seamlessly with smart home devices.

Sound quality is clear and immersive. With Object Tracking Sound Lite, audio feels more dynamic, and pairing it with a soundbar makes it even better.

Overall, the Samsung U7900F offers excellent value: a beautiful design, vivid 4K picture, useful smart functions, and reliable performance. I highly recommend it to anyone looking for a high-quality TV at a great price.

title: Great product
text:
The Samsung U7900F is a sleek, affordable 4K smart TV with vibrant colors, HDR10 support, and a slim metal design. Powered by the Crystal Processor 4K, it delivers sharp detail and smooth performance for everyday streaming and casual viewing. Smart features like Samsung TV Plus (free channels), SmartThings integration, and Gaming Hub add convenience.

Pros: Stylish design, sharp 4K upscaling, solid color and contrast, free streaming channels, lightweight and easy setup.
Cons: No HDMI 2.1, VRR, or 120Hz for gamers; limited black depth (no local dimming); Tizen OS menus can feel clunky.

Verdict: Great for streaming and everyday use in well-lit rooms, but not the best choice for serious gamers or movie enthusiasts who want deep contras.

title: Samsung Delivers Defective & Damaged TV’s
text: I ordered this TV directly from Samsung on a Verizon/Samsung Promotion, and their shipping company RXO delivered a Defective TV that didn’t even look like the one on the box. The screen was pixelated, blurry and unwatchable. It took three weeks for them to even pick it up for return. I had to write to the President and CEO of Samsung to get them to do anything. After the return was finally complete, I reordered the same TV and this one was delivered damaged. It is very interesting as the box and packing material is undamaged, yet the TV is. I am still waiting for them to contact me, but the top people of Samsung Corporation seem to have an “I don’t care” attitude, and they are ignoring my situation. I will think twice about buying another Samsung product. Buyer Beware!

title: Broken TV, Device Confusion, Bad Return Policies
text:
I would give zero stars if I could. This TV appeared at my doorstep damaged with large lines across the screen. I reported this immediately to support, and they approved a return. However, the return could only be done within two weeks of this receipt, and only to one return provider. 

I asked if I could return it directly to a store and get a new version and was told that it was not possible. We also realized that the controller did not interact well with our Samsung computer monitor, so having the TV meant that we would never know which device would turn on and off with the remote. 

Between a family emergency and regular life, my boyfriend and I were not able to return this to the shipping location (that was 30 minutes away from us) in time. I attempted to contact support again to see if there's another option to ensure we get a working TV, and they said no. So- if you order this product, be aware of the following things:

1) If you have other Samsung products, the remotes may cross and turn on the other devices. If your boyfriend is a gamer like mine, this will be a big deal. 
2) If the product arrives damaged, you have a short return window to get it replaced, and only one option to return. If you cannot meet that, you are completely out the money for the TV with no other options. 

Buy another brand and don't waste your time.

title: Stunning Picture & Seamless Performance
text:
I absolutely love my Samsung Crystal UHD TV! The picture quality is incredible—vivid colors, sharp resolution, and smooth motion, whether I’m watching movies, sports, or gaming. The 4K clarity makes a noticeable difference, and HDR adds stunning depth to scenes, especially in dark or brightly lit moments.

I mainly use it for streaming shows and movies through apps like Netflix, Hulu, and Disney+, and the Smart TV interface is very intuitive and user-friendly. Setup was a breeze, and I was connected to Wi-Fi and streaming within minutes. The TV also has multiple HDMI ports, which makes it easy to hook up a soundbar and gaming console without hassle.

One of my favorite features is the voice control—it’s convenient when I’m multitasking or can’t find the remote. I also appreciate the sleek, modern design; it looks fantastic on my wall and elevates the room.

Overall, this TV has greatly improved my daily routine by making downtime more enjoyable and relaxing. I’d recommend it to anyone looking for top-notch performance at an affordable price point.

title: Great Picture / Great Service!
text:
We were really looking forward to getting this new Samsung TV.  We especially liked the Samsung brand because the one we replaced was a Samsung 50" that was about 10 years old. It was still working fine but did not have all the new streaming/smart features. This new Samsung is so bright and clear it is way beyond anything we expected. The new features give us many more streaming choices too. 

I guess I am not supposed to mention customer service but not doing so would leave out one of the most important features of this product. My wife and I are not very tech savvy and so we had to contact Samsung customer service while trying to set up our new TV. Let me just say they were absolutely fantastic. I was never on hold more than about 5 minutes and every person I talked with was courteous and knowledgeable. All our questions were answered quickly and completely. This added so much to our customer and product experience. Our new TV is functioning perfectly. We have complete confidence in this Samsung product and customer service and would unequivocally recommend both!

title: TV was broken when it arrived. Shipped by Samsung
text:
I never got to enjoy my TV. When it arrived, it was broken during shipping. And when I took it out of the box, the screen was shattered. I got the TV as a free promotion from Verizon for signing up with home Internet. I checked with Verizon first and they told me to contact Samsung for help. 
I reached out to Samsung support and they asked me to send pictures of the shattered screen. I work 80 hours a week so it took me 3 days to send the pictures back on the text messages. It was also 4th of July, so a lot of moving pieces going on. The reps I spoke with did not tell me, hey you better respond within 10 hours….needless to say, by the time they got my pictures on the 3rd day, they said the coverage for something they would help with  had expired and I must have broken the TV myself. I could pay for someone to come inspect it and fix it. I kept explaining that I should not have to pay because it was damaged during shipping. I spoke to supervisors and other countless reps but no one seemed to care. This whole transaction was the worst experience I’ve had as an adult, in 41 years. I did not break the TV, it was shipped to my work in a flimsy box with no support inside. I will never do any business with Samsung again. I am very disappointed by the whole thing and don’t want anything to do with the Samsung brand nor their process. What a terrible experience and a waste of time and resources. I should have selected the free oculus gift instead of the giant broken TV. Thank you all.

title: Amazing Picture Quality & Great Value!
text:
I've been using the Samsung U7900F Crystal UHD TV (2025) for a while now and I’m very impressed. The picture quality is vibrant, sharp, and clear—even in brightly lit rooms. The colors really pop, and the 4K upscaling makes everything look better.

Setup was super easy, and the smart features are fast and responsive. Streaming apps like Netflix and YouTube run smoothly, and the remote is user-friendly. Sound quality is decent out of the box, though I paired it with a soundbar for a fuller experience.

Overall, it’s a fantastic TV for the price—perfect for movies, gaming, and everyday use. Highly recommended!

title: U7900F Crystal UHD TV (2025)
text:
I recently bought the 2025 Samsung U7900F Crystal UHD TV, and I’m very happy with it! It’s a perfect fit for my room—the screen is big, but the design is super thin and modern, which looks great on the wall or TV stand.

The picture quality is crystal clear, with beautiful colors and sharp details. It also has amazing smart features that are fast and easy to use. Streaming apps work smoothly, and everything feels very high-tech.

Another big plus is energy efficiency. This TV helps save on electricity bills, which is a great bonus!

Overall, the U7900F is stylish, powerful, and smart—highly recommended!
